data.department_name: Wydział Informatyki i Nauki o Materiałach

visualization.title: Average Overall Evaluation Rating by Department

resultRaw.sql:
SELECT d.name AS department_name,
       ROUND(AVG(ce.overall_rating)::numeric, 2) AS avg_overall_rating
FROM public.course_evaluations ce
JOIN public.enrollments e ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
JOIN public.courses c ON cs.course_id = c.id
JOIN public.departments d ON c.department_id = d.id
GROUP BY d.name
ORDER BY avg_overall_rating DESC, department_name ASC;

issues: Both encoded fields (department_name, avg_overall_rating) exist in metadata.columns — OK.
The chart shows aggregated avg_overall_rating per department (SQL groups by department_name) — OK.

Vega-Lite sort uses {field: avg_overall_rating, op: "average"}, which is unnecessary because the query already returns one aggregated row per department; remove the op or set sort based on the field only to avoid confusion.
The SQL ORDER BY has a secondary sort (department_name ASC) for tie-breaks; the visualization sort does not implement that secondary sort, so tie ordering could differ from the SQL result.
SQL rounds AVG(...) to 2 decimals and the tooltip formats to .2f — this is consistent, but be aware of duplicate rounding at both SQL and viz layers (harmless).

If departments can share identical names in the source data, grouping by name (rather than department ID) could merge distinct departments — consider grouping/labeling by a unique id or code if applicable.

data.department_name: Wydział Informatyki i Nauki o Materiałach

visualization.title: Average Overall Evaluation Rating by Department

result.sql:
SELECT d.name AS department_name,
       ROUND(AVG(ce.overall_rating)::numeric, 2) AS avg_overall_rating
FROM public.course_evaluations ce
JOIN public.enrollments e ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
JOIN public.courses c ON cs.course_id = c.id
JOIN public.departments d ON c.department_id = d.id
GROUP BY d.name
ORDER BY avg_overall_rating DESC, department_name ASC;

issues: Both encoded fields (department_name, avg_overall_rating) exist in metadata.columns — OK.
